title: FGAP-090 orientation block directs the agent to `/context start`, which is NOT a registered subcommand — mis-guides to a non-existent command (conductor is FGAP-095-pending)
description: The FGAP-090 eager orientation block (orientation.ts:36, injected via before_agent_start) tells the in-pi agent 'Bootstrap / onboarding: run /context start'. But /context start is NOT a registered subcommand — the registered CONTEXT_SUBCOMMANDS are init/install/accept-all/view/lens-curate/roadmap-*/status/add-work/validate/help (no 'start'; per the 2026-05-25 explore agent). The /context start unified conductor is DEC-0042-enacted but FGAP-095 P2-P6-pending (only P1 deriveBootstrapState landed). So a fresh agent following the orientation directs the user to a command that returns 'unknown subcommand'. The real current ceremony is /context init <dir> -> /context accept-all -> /context install (three separate subcommands). Same guidance-accuracy class as FGAP-106 (.project leak): the eager guidance describes aspirational/wrong surface vs the live one.

evidence.reference: '- Bootstrap / onboarding: run `/context start`.' — references an unregistered command (shipped in FGAP-090/TASK-074)

CONTEXT_SUBCOMMANDS registration has no 'start' key (init/install/accept-all/view/lens-curate/roadmap-list/roadmap-view/roadmap-validate/status/add-work/validate/help); the real ceremony is init -> accept-all -> install (per the explore agent enumeration)

impact: A fresh constrained agent, steered by the orientation block, tells the user to 'run /context start' — which fails (unknown subcommand). The keystone onboarding guidance points at vapor; mis-onboards every fresh user until /context start is built or the orientation names the real ceremony.
proposed_resolution: Until FGAP-095 builds the /context start conductor, change the orientation block (and any tool/SKILL text referencing /context start) to name the ACTUAL current bootstrap: /context init <dir> -> /context accept-all -> /context install. When FGAP-095 lands /context start, switch the orientation to it (single entry per DEC-0042). Pairs with FGAP-106 (same eager-guidance-accuracy surface) + FGAP-090 + FGAP-095 + DEC-0042. Verify via a live constrained run that the orientation names a command that actually resolves.
